content: Which from following quantity of different gases contains maximum number of molecules?
explanation:
Number of molecules

$=$ Number of moles $\times 6.022 \times 10^{23}$

Number of molecules $\propto$ Number of moles

(A) $\quad \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{N}_2}=\frac{7}{28}=0.25 \mathrm{~mol}$

(B) $\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2}=\frac{2}{2}=1 \mathrm{~mol}$

(C) $\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{O}_2}=\frac{8}{32}=0.25 \mathrm{~mol}$

(D) $\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{NO}_2}=\frac{20}{46}=0.43 \mathrm{~mol}$

Thus, $\mathrm{H}_2$ amongst given $2 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{H}_2$ gas will contain maximum number of molecules.

content: Which from following is an example of intensive property?
explanation: Intensive property is independent of the amount of matter in a system. Mass, internal energy, and the number of moles are examples of extensive property and density is an example of intensive property.

content: 24 g carbon react with oxygen to produce 88 g carbon dioxide. Find the amount of oxygen used.
explanation:
$$ \text { Reaction can be given as: } $$

Molar masses:

$\mathrm{C}=12 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol} ; \mathrm{CO}_2=44 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol} ; \mathrm{O}_2=32 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}$

Given: 24 g carbon $=\frac{24}{12}=2 \mathrm{~mol}$ of Carbon

$88 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CO}_2=\frac{88}{44}=2 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $\mathrm{CO}_2$

From the reaction, 1 mol of carbon reacts with 1 mol of $\mathrm{O}_2$ to produce 1 mol of $\mathrm{CO}_2$. Thus, 2 mol carbon requires $2 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{O}_2$.

$\therefore \quad$ Mass of $\mathrm{O}_2=2 \times 32=64 \mathrm{~g}$

content: What is the percentage by mass of oxygen in water, if percentage by mass of hydrogen is $11.1 \%$ ?
explanation:
Let the total mass of water be $100 \ \text{g}$.

Given: Percentage by mass of hydrogen $= 11.1\%$

So, mass of hydrogen in $100 \ \text{g}$ water $= 11.1 \ \text{g}$

Mass of oxygen in $100 \ \text{g}$ water:

$ = 100 \ \text{g} - 11.1 \ \text{g} = 88.9 \ \text{g} $

Therefore, percentage by mass of oxygen:

$ = \frac{\text{Mass of oxygen}}{\text{Total mass}} \times 100\% = \frac{88.9}{100} \times 100\% = 88.9\% $

Correct Option:

Option D $88.9\%$

content: What is the ratio of mass of nitrogen that combines with 16 parts by weight of oxygen in $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{NO}_2$ ?
explanation:
In each oxide, calculate the mass of N that combines with 16 g of O:

• N₂O

– Molar masses: N₂ = 2×14 = 28, O = 16

– So 28 g N combines with 16 g O.

• NO

– N = 14, O = 16

– So 14 g N combines with 16 g O.

• NO₂

– N = 14, O₂ = 2×16 = 32

– In one mole NO₂, 14 g N combines with 32 g O.

– For 16 g O:

$$\text{N mass} = 14 \times \frac{16}{32} = 7\text{ g}.$$

Write the masses of N per 16 g O:

N₂O : NO : NO₂ = 28 : 14 : 7

Simplify the ratio by dividing all terms by 7:

28 : 14 : 7 = 4 : 2 : 1

Answer: 4 : 2 : 1
